description: A schema for files contained within the unified validation/ test suite

items.description: An individual test case, containing multiple tests of a single schema's behavior across dialects

comment.description: Any additional comments about the test case

compatibility.description: Dialect constraint for this test case. A bare number means 'that dialect and above' (minimum). Prefix with '=' for an exact match or '<=' for a maximum. Combine with commas for a range, e.g. '6,<=2019'. Valid dialect tokens: 3, 4, 6, 7, 2019, 2020.

schema.description: A valid JSON Schema, written without a $schema keyword so it stays dialect-agnostic. The applicable dialects are expressed via the compatibility field.

tests.description: A set of related tests all using the same schema

specification.description: A reference to a specification document which defines the behavior tested by this test case. Typically this should be a JSON Schema specification document, though in cases where the JSON Schema specification points to another RFC it should contain *both* the portion of the JSON Schema specification which indicates what RFC (and section) to follow as *well* as information on where in that specification the behavior is specified.

core.description: A section in official JSON Schema core drafts

validation.description: A section in official JSON Schema validation drafts

ecma262.description: A section in official ECMA 262 specification for defining regular expressions

perl5.description: A section name in Perl documentation for defining regular expressions

^rfc\\d+$.description: A section in official RFC for the given rfc number

^iso\\d+$.description: A section in official ISO for the given iso number

description.description: The test description, briefly explaining which behavior it exercises

comment.description: Any additional comments about the test

data.description: The instance which should be validated against the schema in "schema".

valid.description: Whether the validation process of this instance should consider the instance valid or not
